# VeloIQ™ Framework

> Open-core full-stack framework for building data-driven admin and ERP applications. Define SQLModel models in Python; the framework auto-generates REST API, SQLAdmin back-office, and React CRUD frontend.

Built on FastAPI + SQLModel (backend) and React + Refine + Ant Design (frontend). Pro-code, not no-code — generated code is yours to read, extend, and override.

Website: https://veloiq.dev
Company: JuiceMantics
License: MIT — Copyright (c) 2026 JuiceMantics

---

## Quick start

```bash
pip install veloiq-framework
veloiq new my-app
cd my-app/backend && cp .env.example .env   # set DATABASE_URL
veloiq db upgrade
veloiq run                                  # http://localhost:8000
cd ../frontend && npm install && npm run dev # http://localhost:5173
```

---

## Repository layout

```
backend/
  veloiq_framework/         # pip package: veloiq-framework
    auth/                   # User, Role, Tenant models + JWT + RBAC
    cli/                    # veloiq CLI (new, add-module, generate, run, db)
    scaffold/               # templates for `veloiq new`
  pyproject.toml
packages/
  ui/                       # npm package: @veloiq/ui (React components)
    src/
    dist/                   # committed — consumers install from here
docs/                       # full documentation
samples/task-manager/       # complete reference application
```

---

## CLI commands

```bash
veloiq new <app>              # scaffold a new project
veloiq add-module <name>      # add a module to an existing project
veloiq generate               # regenerate api.py + TypeScript schemas from models
veloiq run                    # start uvicorn dev server (auto-detects app/main.py)
veloiq db upgrade             # apply Alembic migrations
veloiq db migrate -m "msg"    # create a new migration revision
veloiq db history             # show revision history
veloiq db current             # show current revision
```

---

## Project structure (generated by `veloiq new`)

```
my-app/
├── backend/
│   ├── app/
│   │   ├── main.py               # one line: app = create_veloiq_app()
│   │   └── modules/
│   │       └── <name>/
│   │           ├── models.py     # YOU WRITE THIS
│   │           ├── api.py        # AUTO-GENERATED — do not edit
│   │           ├── custom_api.py # custom endpoints — you write this
│   │           └── admin/
│   │               └── admin_views.py  # SQLAdmin views — you write this
│   ├── .env
│   ├── requirements.txt
│   └── api_schema_gen.py
└── frontend/
    └── src/
        ├── App.tsx               # app shell — you can customise
        └── allModels.gen.ts      # AUTO-GENERATED — do not edit
```

**Never edit `api.py` or `*.gen.ts` files.** They are overwritten by `veloiq generate`.

---

## Backend entry point (main.py)

Minimal — one line:
```python
from veloiq_framework import create_veloiq_app
app = create_veloiq_app()
```

With config:
```python
from veloiq_framework import (
    create_veloiq_app, VeloIQConfig,
    RoleDef, ALL_METHODS, WRITE_METHODS, READ_METHODS,
)

app = create_veloiq_app(VeloIQConfig(
    title="My App",
    roles=[
        RoleDef("Admin",   ALL_METHODS,   "Full access",         is_preset=True),
        RoleDef("Manager", WRITE_METHODS, "Create/edit, no delete", is_preset=True),
        RoleDef("Viewer",  READ_METHODS,  "Read-only",           is_preset=True),
    ],
))
```

---

## Models

### Base classes

```python
from veloiq_framework import FrameworkModel, TimestampedModel

# FrameworkModel — id primary key only, no timestamps
class Category(FrameworkModel, table=True):
    __tablename__ = "category"
    name: str

# TimestampedModel — id + created_at + updated_at (appended last in UI)
class Product(TimestampedModel, table=True):
    __tablename__ = "product"
    name: str
    price: float
    in_stock: bool = True
    description: Optional[str] = None
```

### Relations

Use `jm_relationship()` (drop-in for SQLModel `Relationship`):

```python
from typing import List, Optional
from sqlmodel import Field
from veloiq_framework import TimestampedModel, jm_relationship

class Customer(TimestampedModel, table=True):
    __tablename__ = "customer"
    name: str
    orders: List["Order"] = jm_relationship(back_populates="customer")

class Order(TimestampedModel, table=True):
    __tablename__ = "order"
    reference: str
    customer_id: Optional[int] = Field(default=None, foreign_key="customer.id")
    customer: Optional["Customer"] = jm_relationship(back_populates="orders")
```

After editing `models.py`, always run `veloiq generate` then `veloiq db upgrade`.

---

## Access control

### Layer 1 — Configurable roles (global, in main.py)

```python
RoleDef("Admin",   ALL_METHODS,   "Full access",    is_preset=True)
RoleDef("Manager", WRITE_METHODS, "No delete",      is_preset=True)
RoleDef("Viewer",  READ_METHODS,  "Read-only",      is_preset=True)
RoleDef("Auditor", READ_METHODS,  "External",       is_preset=True)
# ALL_METHODS    = ["list","show","create","edit","delete","clone","field"]
# WRITE_METHODS  = ["list","show","create","edit","clone","field"]
# READ_METHODS   = ["list","show","field"]
```

Roles are seeded to the DB on startup and editable at runtime via the admin UI.

### Layer 2 — Model-level exceptions (`@model_access`)

Narrows a role's permissions on one specific model. Cannot grant beyond global:

```python
from veloiq_framework import model_access, TimestampedModel

@model_access(Viewer=["list", "show"])   # Viewer gets read-only on Invoice
class Invoice(TimestampedModel, table=True):
    __tablename__ = "invoice"
    amount: float
    status: str = "draft"
```

### Layer 3 — Field-level exceptions (`veloiq_field`)

Controls read/write access per field. Uses `pydantic.Field` under the hood:

```python
from veloiq_framework import veloiq_field, TimestampedModel

class Employee(TimestampedModel, table=True):
    __tablename__ = "employee"
    name: str
    salary: float = veloiq_field(default=0.0, read_roles=["Admin"], write_roles=["Admin"])
    notes: str = veloiq_field(default="", write_roles=["Admin", "Manager"])
```

Run `veloiq generate` after changing `veloiq_field` annotations — it emits
`readRoles`/`writeRoles` into the TypeScript schema so the UI hides/disables
restricted fields automatically.

### ReBAC — Row-level access control (`@rebac`)

Filters which rows a user can see/edit/delete based on data ownership or relationships:

```python
from veloiq_framework import rebac, rebac_subquery, TimestampedModel
from sqlmodel import Field

# Ownership: user sees only their own rows
@rebac(owner_field="created_by")
class Note(TimestampedModel, table=True):
    __tablename__ = "note"
    created_by: int = Field(foreign_key="veloiq_user.id")
    body: str

# Tenant isolation
@rebac(tenant_field="tenant_id")
class Contract(TimestampedModel, table=True):
    __tablename__ = "contract"
    tenant_id: int = Field(foreign_key="veloiq_tenant.id")

# Relationship traversal (inherit access from parent)
@rebac(filter=lambda user, cls, session:
           cls.folder_id.in_(rebac_subquery(Folder, user, session)))
class Document(TimestampedModel, table=True):
    __tablename__ = "document"
    folder_id: int
```

Rules:
- `@rebac` applies to **all roles** including Admin
- Inaccessible rows return **404**, not 403
- Multiple patterns on one decorator are **OR-combined**
- `rebac_subquery()` target model must itself have `@rebac`; circular refs raise `ValueError`

---

## Custom endpoints (custom_api.py)

Import `router` from the generated `api.py` and add routes to it:

```python
from fastapi import Depends, HTTPException
from sqlmodel import Session, select
from veloiq_framework import get_session
from .api import router
from .models import Order

@router.post("/{order_id}/confirm")
def confirm_order(order_id: int, session: Session = Depends(get_session)):
    order = session.get(Order, order_id)
    if order is None:
        raise HTTPException(404)
    order.status = "confirmed"
    session.add(order)
    session.commit()
    session.refresh(order)
    return order.model_dump()
```

The framework loader imports `custom_api.py` automatically — no registration needed.

---

## Admin views (admin/admin_views.py)

```python
from sqladmin import ModelView
from app.modules.orders.models import Order

class OrderAdmin(ModelView, model=Order):
    column_list = [Order.id, Order.reference, Order.status, Order.total]
    column_searchable_list = [Order.reference]
    column_sortable_list = [Order.total, Order.created_at]
    icon = "fa-solid fa-receipt"
```

Admin panel is at `http://localhost:8000/admin/`.

---

## VeloIQConfig reference

| Field | Env var | Default | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `database_url` | `DATABASE_URL` | — | Required. SQLAlchemy URL. |
| `title` | — | `"VeloIQ App"` | Shown in `/docs` |
| `modules_dir` | `VELOIQ_MODULES_DIR` | `"app/modules"` | Relative to CWD |
| `cors_origins` | `CORS_ORIGINS` | `["*"]` | Comma-separated in env |
| `auth_enabled` | `VELOIQ_AUTH_DISABLED` | `True` | Set `VELOIQ_AUTH_DISABLED=1` to skip auth in dev |
| `auth_secret` | `AUTH_SECRET` | — | Required in production |
| `auth_token_expire_minutes` | `AUTH_TOKEN_EXPIRE_MINUTES` | `480` | |
| `admin_username` | `VELOIQ_ADMIN_USERNAME` | `"admin"` | Initial seed only |
| `admin_password` | `VELOIQ_ADMIN_PASSWORD` | `"admin"` | Initial seed only |
| `echo_sql` | `VELOIQ_ECHO_SQL` | `False` | Log SQL to stdout |
| `create_tables_on_startup` | — | `True` | Set `False` when using Alembic |
| `rebac_enabled` | `VELOIQ_REBAC_ENABLED` | `False` | Enable row-level filtering |
| `roles` | — | `DEFAULT_ROLES` | `list[RoleDef]` |

---

## Auth tables

Built-in auth uses these table names (prefixed to avoid collision):

| Table | Model |
|---|---|
| `veloiq_user` | `User` — username, email, status, roles, tenants |
| `veloiq_role` | `Role` — name, allowed_methods, is_preset |
| `veloiq_tenant` | `Tenant` — name, domain, status |
| `veloiq_user_has_role` | M2M join |
| `veloiq_user_has_tenant` | M2M join |

Reference auth tables in foreign keys as `"veloiq_user.id"`, `"veloiq_role.id"`, etc.

---

## Generated CRUD endpoints

`create_crud_router(Model)` produces:

| Method | Path | Action |
|---|---|---|
| GET | `/{resource}` | Paginated list (`?_start=0&_end=25`) |
| GET | `/{resource}/{id}` | Single record |
| POST | `/{resource}` | Create |
| PUT | `/{resource}/{id}` | Full update |
| DELETE | `/{resource}/{id}` | Delete |

Responses include an `eid` field (alias for `id`) for frontend compatibility.
List responses set `x-total-count` and `content-range` headers for Refine pagination.

---

## Frontend (@veloiq/ui)

```tsx
import {
    authProvider, accessControlProvider, httpClient,
    LayoutWrapper, LoginPage,
    DynamicList, DynamicShow, DynamicCreate, DynamicEdit,
    generateResources, authSystemModels,
    MultiPaneLayout, ColorModeContextProvider,
} from "@veloiq/ui";
```

Key components:
- `DynamicList / DynamicShow / DynamicCreate / DynamicEdit` — schema-driven CRUD pages
- `generateResources(models, moduleName)` — builds Refine resource definitions
- `authSystemModels` — ModelDef array for User / Role / Tenant pages
- `MultiPaneLayout` — side-panel layout; click a list row to open detail beside it
- `LoginPage` — takes `appTitle` prop
- `LayoutWrapper` — takes `appTitle` prop; wraps authenticated routes

---

## Key conventions

- **Never edit `api.py` or `*.gen.ts`** — both are overwritten by `veloiq generate`
- **`jm_relationship()`** is the correct relationship helper (not SQLModel's `Relationship`)
- **`TimestampedModel`** appends `created_at` / `updated_at` after all declared fields
- **`FrameworkModel`** if you don't want timestamps
- **`veloiq_field()`** instead of `Field()` when you need role-scoped read/write
- After every model change: `veloiq generate` → `veloiq db upgrade`
- Module discovery is automatic — drop files in `app/modules/<name>/` and they're live
- `custom_api.py` must import `router` from `api.py` in the same module
- Set `VELOIQ_AUTH_DISABLED=1` in `.env` during early development to skip auth

---

## Public API exports (veloiq_framework)

```python
from veloiq_framework import (
    # App factory
    create_veloiq_app, VeloIQConfig,
    # Models
    FrameworkModel, TimestampedModel, StandardModel,
    jm_relationship, get_pk_field_name, RelationCardinality,
    # Database
    get_session,
    # CRUD
    create_crud_router,
    # Auth utilities
    get_current_user, require_role,
    # RBAC
    RoleDef, model_access, veloiq_field,
    ALL_METHODS, WRITE_METHODS, READ_METHODS, DEFAULT_ROLES,
    # ReBAC
    rebac, rebac_subquery,
    # Version
    __version__,
)
```
